01 / About

Grounded in roots,
driven by growth.

I graduated from Fordham with a major in Finance and a minor in Economics. I've built skills in financial analysis and data interpretation, and I'm genuinely passionate about the dynamics of markets and what drives value creation in the finance industry.

Growing up, I spent countless hours at my parents' small jewelry shop in New York. Helping with simple tasks taught me the importance of customer relationships and the value of hard work. Those experiences sparked my interest in business operations and finance, which shaped my appreciation for both the analytical and interpersonal sides of the industry.

Today I want to leverage that combination: rigor with numbers, and the human side of building meaningful connections. It's the same instinct whether I'm reading the tape before the open or partnering with a client to solve a problem.

03b / Selected wins

Two problems, in depth

Research & Analytics · MetroPlusHealth

Helping flag high-risk members earlier

Summer 2024 · Behavioral Health team
ChallengeThe Behavioral Health team needed a better way to identify high-risk HARP members from a sprawling set of healthcare records, accuracy and compliance were non-negotiable.
What I didI used SQL to manage and organize a dataset of more than a million records, verified 500+ credentialing records with a zero error rate, and supported the analysis that fed a patient risk-scoring model.
+20%improvement in identifying high-risk members
FP&A & Operations · Golden Palace Jewelry

Tighter forecasts, leaner inventory

2025 · $100K+ monthly volume
ChallengeA multi-location retail business needed more accurate forecasting and smarter inventory planning to support growth while controlling waste and cost.
What I didI owned FP&A across six-figure monthly volume, refined the forecasting and budgeting assumptions, ran reconciliations and POS audits, and managed AI-driven inventory initiatives and vendor negotiations.
+20%forecast accuracy, with month-over-month revenue gains
